note_1_nature_of_operations.entity_history: Organized 2014-11-14 as Utah corporation; converted to Utah LLC 2016-12-31
note_1_nature_of_operations.business: Franchise company for Cupbop Co.; grants franchisees the right to operate physical storefront and/or mobile food truck under Cupbop name and marks

key_estimates: allowances for credit losses on accounts receivable
use_of_estimates.note: Current economic environment increases uncertainty; pervasive throughout statements

accounts_receivable_policy.terms: 30 days net; past 120 days = delinquent; interest accrues until past one year then ceases

contract_liabilities_policy.definition: Obligation to transfer goods/services where consideration already received; primarily deferred franchise fee revenues

long_lived_asset_impairment_policy.trigger: Events or circumstances indicating carrying amount may not be recoverable
long_lived_asset_impairment_policy.test: Undiscounted estimated cash flows vs carrying amount; impairment loss = excess of carrying over fair value

revenue_recognition.initial_franchise_fees: Approximately 50% recognized upon franchise opening and completion of related training; remaining 50% recognized straight-line over expected 10-year contract life
revenue_recognition.if_location_closes_early: Remaining unearned revenue recognized into income at time of closure
revenue_recognition.royalties: 6% of monthly gross sales recognized monthly under sales-based royalty exception
revenue_recognition.renewal_fees: Recognized as income when renewal agreement becomes effective

income_taxes_policy.structure: LLC member elected to be taxed as a C-corp under IRC; income/loss reported to individual member
income_taxes_policy.ptet_election: Pass-through entity tax (PTET) paid at entity level on behalf of member in Utah
income_taxes_policy.ptet_treatment: Accounted for as distribution to member for financial-statement purposes; deducted on federal return

concentrations_of_risk.customer_concentration: Majority of A/R from one customer in both FY2024 and FY2023

note_2_related_party_transactions.basis: Marketing revenues earned by Company before cash received by parent; due on demand; non-interest-bearing
note_2_related_party_transactions.ap_basis: Prior-year company expenses paid by parent; non-interest-bearing; due on demand

key_facts_promotable_to_canonical: marketing_income_pct_of_revenue_2024 = 41% — meaning marketing fund accounting flows nearly half of total franchisor revenue (royalties + FFE comprise the other ~59%)

advertising_expense_2024 = $285,153 vs marketing_income_2024 ~$828,000 — net marketing fund surplus of ~$543,000 retained or used for non-direct-advertising purposes (consistent with the 40% admin allocation in Item 11)
ptet_payments_2024 = $79,245 included in distributions — recharacterizes the headline distribution figure
useful_life_3_years for both website development and furniture/fixtures
0 impairments and 0 credit losses in 2024 and 2023
majority of A/R from one customer (concentration risk)
initial franchise fee revenue recognition: ~50% at opening + training, 50% straight-line over 10-year contract life
